geographic_restrictions: Global, with specific restrictions for countries under U.S. embargoes/sanctions (e.g., Cuba, Iran, Syria, North Korea, Crimea). Projects must comply with U.S. export laws and obtain necessary permits.
project_area: Science, conservation, storytelling, education, technology, climate resilience, cultural heritage preservation, community-based stewardship, and ecosystem restoration.
demographic_requirements: Applicants must be 18 or older. Ideal candidates have a strong connection to the local context (e.g., members of local communities, living near fieldwork areas, speaking local languages, or having long-standing relationships with stakeholders).
eligibility_summary: Open to individuals globally who are 18+. Applicants must demonstrate a strong understanding of and connection to the local context of their project. Only one proposal per project leader at a time. Previous grantees must submit final reports and media before applying for new projects. Compliance with U.S. laws (e.g., anti-bribery, sanctions, export controls) is mandatory.
